title: Multivariate OOF Calibrator for Diverse Experts
summary: Calibration papers show beta calibration, ENIR, and multivariate isotonic methods can outperform a single sigmoid when combining distorted scores from different base learners.
rationale: Calibration papers show beta calibration, ENIR, and multivariate isotonic methods can outperform a single sigmoid when combining distorted scores from different base learners. That is relevant if you blend a pointwise booster, lap-ranker, and regime expert whose score shapes differ.
implementation_hint: Generate strict race-held-out OOF predictions from 2-4 diverse models, then fit a tiny second-stage calibrator on score vectors only: start with beta-calibrated logistic stacking, compare against ENIR, and optionally test a 2D monotone calibrator on the two strongest models. Prefer the simplest variant if OOF AUC gains are tiny.
expected_effect: Small but plausible lift from exploiting complementary score geometry while keeping submissions more stable across race regimes.
risk: Nonparametric calibrators can overfit badly with only 26 grouped events, so keep the meta-model low-capacity and fully OOF.
